threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always spot a newbie—they're the ones still using the map to find their way around London Fields Park on a sunny Saturday.
Last week on the N155, a guy in a parrot costume kept arguing with the driver about the best route to Brixton. Only in South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a cat fight at 3 AM, followed by a neighbor yelling for their missing pants. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 4 AM, then stumble into an underground club where the DJ is also the bartender. Pure Berlin magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name at least three taquerias on a first-name basis and have strong opinions about the salsa.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a Muni bus stop as a guy juggled flaming torches on the corner. Everyone cheered, then the bus left without him. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all tapas are the same, but each bar in Gràcia has its own twist. You’ll find everything from spicy bombes to classic patatas braves.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered look while trying to navigate the open-air markets, clutching a guidebook like a lifeline.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 24-hour bus is a luxury, not just a lifeline for late-night post-pub trips to get your Serotonin fix.
Last night, a guy got on the 59 in Brixton, ordered a curry delivery mid-journey, then offered it to the driver. Classic London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was truly living in Neukölln when I saw a guy selling homemade currywurst from a bicycle on a rainy Tuesday. Only here!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a laundromat that doubles as a bar, where you can sip craft beer while waiting for your clothes to wash.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can describe the exact moment when Muni delays hit peak frustration—often around the same time as a burrito craving.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 at Civic Center yesterday just as a dog decided to take a leisurely stroll on the tracks. Only in SF would this be a daily highlight.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best tapas spots are always crowded; they miss the hidden gems where locals still chat with the owner over a glass of vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their hesitant steps at Pl. del Sol, trying to decipher the Spanish menu while holding a full-size map like it’s a lifebuoy.
